text: It is as if I have entered a threshold between the mundane and the contemplative. The first scene—a quiet, empty passage with soft light and utilitarian objects—feels liminal, sterile, suspended in expectation. The second scene, overwhelmed by visual stimuli and words hinting at research, development, and human-machine coexistence, pulls me into the pulse of progress. I am intrigued, slightly disoriented by the tilt and color, but also curious. Here, amidst curated thought and innovation, I sense my mind reaching outward—toward possibility and connection—even as my feet stand unsure in the present.
